title: [Bug]: Inconsistent results between row storage and column storage for the same query when using `NULLIF`.
description: <!-- A clear and concise description of bug. -->
poc: SELECT /*+ NO_USE_COLUMN_TABLE(t0) */ ALL CONCAT(t0.c0, ''), t0.c1, t0.c2
SELECT /*+ USE_COLUMN_TABLE(t0) */ ALL CONCAT(t0.c0, ''), t0.c1, t0.c2
EXPLAIN SELECT /*+ NO_USE_COLUMN_TABLE(t0) */ ALL CONCAT(t0.c0, ''), t0.c1, t0.c2
EXPLAIN SELECT /*+ USE_COLUMN_TABLE(t0) */ ALL CONCAT(t0.c0, ''), t0.c1, t0.c2
key_bug_features: Inconsistent results using NULLIF with row and column storage
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE `test_table` (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value1 DOUBLE, value2 DOUBLE, value3 VARCHAR(10));
INSERT INTO `test_table` (id, value1, value2, value3) VALUES (1, 0.0, 0.1, 'test1'), (2, 0.1, NULL, 'test2');
SELECT /*+ NO_USE_COLUMN_TABLE(test_table) */ value1, value2, NULLIF(value1, value3) AS result FROM `test_table` WHERE value2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT /*+ USE_COLUMN_TABLE(test_table) */ value1, value2, NULLIF(value1, value3) AS result FROM `test_table` WHERE value2 IS NOT NULL;
